results.title: Tropical Storm Bertha to weaken Wednesday
PrintCon 2026 and Print Philippines unifies the Philippine print industry toward stronger future
results.description: PrintCon 2026 and Print Philippines concluded its three‐day unified tradeshow at the SMX Convention Center, marking a historic collaboration between the Philippine Printing Technical Foundation (PPTF) and the Printing Industries Association of the Philippines (PIAP). The event brought together exhibitors, innovators, educators, government agencies, and creative communities under one strengthened national platform. The tradeshow opened with a ribbon‐cutting led by PPTF President Dante E. Llarena and PIAP President Albert See, joined by leaders from major print and packaging organizations. A vibrant Lion and Dragon Dance welcomed guests into the exhibition halls, symbolizing renewed unity across the sector. Government Leaders Affirm Strong Support A key highlight of the event was the message delivered by DTI Undersecretary Blesila A. “Bless” Lantayona, who encouraged the industry to “continue building an industry that is innovative yet responsible, competitive yet inclusive, and profitable yet sustainable.” Her remarks reinforced the government’s commitment to supporting the growth and modernization of the Philippine print and packaging sectors. Additional support came from ASEAN‐BAC Executive Adviser Malyn Molina, who emphasized the role of printing and packaging in the creative economy, and leaders from the Philippine Trade Training Center (PTTC), who reaffirmed their mission to equip MSMEs and professionals with future‐ready skills. Strengthening Collaboration and Industry Trust Keynote speaker Josiah Go, Chairman of Mansmith & Fielders, underscored the importance of “trust infrastructure” in building a unified industry, noting that no single company can elevate Philippine printing alone — only a cohesive, collaborative sector can. The unified PrintCon–PhilPrint format also maximized exhibitor resources by eliminating duplication and strengthening the industry’s shared voice. Education, Creativity, and Global Opportunities Multi‐sectoral partners such as DTI, DOST, SB Corp, NBDB, and TESDA reinforced the event’s educational mission. Creative leaders highlighted growing global opportunities for Filipino comics and printed books, signaling strong international interest in Philippine content. Message from the Conference Chair Ms. Leah Marie Ayeng, PPTF Communications & Research Chair and PrintCon 2026 Conference Chair, shared: “PrintCon 2026 proves that unity and collaboration can move the industry forward. This is only the beginning — our goal is to build on this momentum and elevate Philippine print on the global stage.” About PPTF The Philippine Printing Technical Foundation (PPTF) is the country’s leading institution for print education, technical training, and industry capability building.

results.title: Lovi Poe enters new season of life, career
results.description: Actress, singer, producer, entrepreneur and Hollywood actress Lovi Poe is now officially a Kapamilya after signing with ABS-CBN's Star Magic. The move had been rumored for months, but Tuesday's announcement finally made it official, ushering in what many expect to be an exciting new era for one of the country's most versatile and fearless leading ladies. For someone who has built a career by taking risks, the decision feels like the natural next step. More than the glamour, however, Lovi says she is entering this chapter with a completely different outlook on life. "You do things that make you feel good. I think you just have to do things that make you happy and make you feel good and just don't think about the opinion of others because you want to live your life the way that makes you happy," she told reporters. "That's what I've learned. Sometimes, especially in our industry, we tend to impress others or try to impress a lot of people. At the end of the day, they really won't do anything for you. I think that's one thing that I've learned, to work on myself in the most inward manner." The words carry extra weight coming from an actress who has spent years under the spotlight. Once known as one of the industry's boldest, sexiest and most daring stars, Lovi is now balancing multiple roles, not only as an actress but also as a wife, a mother, a producer and an artist steadily carving out a place in Hollywood. Last October, she welcomed her first child with husband Montgomery Blencowe. Before taking time off for motherhood in the United States, she appeared in the Hollywood film "Bad Man" and starred in "Guilty Pleasure," where her performance earned Best Actress nominations from both the Gawad Urian Awards and PMPC Star Awards for Movies. Despite the growing list of responsibilities, Lovi made it clear that her passion for storytelling remains stronger than ever. "I just want to keep working and doing what I love," she said. At Star Magic's official welcome, she admitted the moment still feels surreal. "It's just nice to be with family now. So I think more than anything, I'm just really grateful." She also paid tribute to her husband, who has supported her career despite the challenges of maintaining a long-distance marriage because of work commitments. "He's been very supportive. He's been traveling back and forth wherever I am. Talagang grateful ako. He's been helping and still wants me to achieve my goals even if it's been hard for us. It takes a real man to be able to make something like that happen." Calling the move "a dream come true," Lovi revealed she grew up watching ABS-CBN and Star Magic artists, never imagining she would someday become part of the family. Star Magic head Laurenti Dyogi described their newest talent as "a true superwoman" who embodies excellence as an actress, singer, performer, producer and storyteller while carrying the legacy of her father, the late Fernando Poe Jr., the legendary King of Philippine Movies. As for what's next, Lovi isn't slowing down. She hopes to headline action projects, thrillers, horror films and comedies while working with Kapamilya stars Kathryn Bernardo, Gerald Anderson, Jericho Rosales, Belle Mariano and Maris Racal. Perhaps her biggest dream, however, is deeply personal. She hopes to remake one of the iconic films of her father, Fernando Poe Jr. "I don't really have to say just one example, but I think an FPJ story would be great. It doesn't matter what it is. I hope that I get to do a project that my dad did." It's a fitting ambition for an actress entering a new phase of her career, one no longer defined by proving herself to others but by choosing projects that inspire her.
